title.en: Ensure bogus icmp responses are ignored

prose.en: Ensure bogus icmp responses are ignored

description.en: Setting `net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses` to `1` prevents the kernel from logging bogus responses (RFC\-1122 non\-compliant) from broadcast reframes, keeping file systems from filling up with useless log messages.

rationale.en: Some routers (and some attackers) will send responses that violate RFC\-1122 and attempt to fill up a log file system with many useless error messages.

audit.en:
Set the following parameter in `/etc/sysctl.conf` or a file in `/etc/sysctl.d/` ending in `.conf`:  

- `net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses = 1`

*Example:*  

Run the following script to set the active kernel parameters:  

**Note:** If these settings appear in a canonically later file, or later in the same file, these settings will be overwritten

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

{
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses=1
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.route.flush=1
}
```

```
# printf '%s\n' "net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses = 1" >> /etc/sysctl.d/60-netipv4_sysctl.conf
```

remediation.en:
Set the following parameter in `/etc/sysctl.conf` or a file in `/etc/sysctl.d/` ending in `.conf`:  

- `net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses = 1`

*Example:*  

Run the following script to set the active kernel parameters:  

**Note:** If these settings appear in a canonically later file, or later in the same file, these settings will be overwritten

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

{
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses=1
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.route.flush=1
}
```

```
# printf '%s\n' "net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses = 1" >> /etc/sysctl.d/60-netipv4_sysctl.conf
```

references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: CM-1,CM-2, CM-6, CM-7, IA-5
